title: Rohan's Silly Sandwich Day
synopsis: Rohan wants to make the perfect sandwich for the school picnic, but everything goes hilariously wrong until his friends help him discover that the silliest sandwich might be the best one of all.

pages.text: Rohan bounced out of bed on Friday morning. Today was the big school picnic! "I'm going to make the most amazing sandwich ever," he told his stuffed elephant, Peanut. Rohan had dark brown skin and curly black hair that stuck up in all directions. His big brown eyes sparkled with excitement. "Everyone will be so impressed!" he said, doing a little dance around his room.

illustration.description: A cheerful South Asian boy with dark brown skin and messy curly black hair jumping excitedly in his colorful bedroom. He's wearing striped pajamas and has big expressive brown eyes. A gray stuffed elephant sits on his bed. Bright morning sunlight streams through the window, casting warm golden light across the room.
illustration.alt: Rohan dancing excitedly in his bedroom with his stuffed elephant

pages.text: At breakfast, Rohan's mom smiled at him. She had the same curly hair and warm brown eyes. "What kind of sandwich are you thinking about?" she asked. "Something AMAZING," Rohan said, spreading his arms wide. "With pickles and peanut butter and maybe some cheese!" His mom laughed. "That sounds... interesting. Are you sure about pickles and peanut butter?" But Rohan was already planning. He could see it now - the perfect, impressive sandwich that would make all his friends say "WOW!"

illustration.description: Rohan sitting at a bright yellow kitchen table with his mom, a South Asian woman with curly black hair in a colorful sari. She's laughing warmly while Rohan gestures dramatically with his arms spread wide. The kitchen is filled with morning sunlight and has colorful bowls and fruits on the counter.
illustration.alt: Rohan talking excitedly to his mom in their sunny kitchen

pages.text: At school, Rohan told his best friend Maya about his sandwich plan. Maya had long black braids and wore bright purple glasses that made her eyes look extra big. "Pickles and peanut butter?" she asked, scrunching up her nose. "Are you sure that will taste good?" "Of course!" Rohan said confidently. "It's going to be the best sandwich at the whole picnic!" Their friend Carlos overheard them talking. Carlos had short brown hair and freckles across his tan cheeks. "I'm bringing my abuela's special ham and cheese," he said. "What about you, Maya?" "Turkey and avocado," Maya replied. "My dad makes it with special spices."

illustration.description: Three diverse children sitting together at school. Rohan gestures enthusiastically while Maya, a girl with long black braids and purple glasses, looks skeptical. Carlos, a Latino boy with brown hair and freckles, listens with interest. They're sitting on colorful classroom chairs with educational posters in the background.
illustration.alt: Rohan talking to his friends Maya and Carlos at school

pages.text: The next morning, Rohan stood in his kitchen ready to make his masterpiece. He had laid out bread, peanut butter, pickles, cheese, bananas, and even some leftover spaghetti from dinner. "This will be EPIC!" he announced to no one in particular. He started spreading peanut butter on the bread. But he squeezed the jar too hard and - SPLURT! - peanut butter went flying everywhere. It landed on his shirt, on the counter, and somehow even on the ceiling. "Oops," Rohan said, looking up at the brown blob above his head.

illustration.description: Rohan in the kitchen covered in peanut butter, with a shocked expression and his mouth open wide. Peanut butter is splattered on his striped shirt, the yellow counter, and there's a brown blob on the white ceiling. Sandwich ingredients are scattered around him on the counter in a chaotic but colorful display.
illustration.alt: Rohan covered in peanut butter after a kitchen accident

pages.text: "It's okay," Rohan said to himself. "I can fix this." He wiped off the peanut butter and tried again. This time he was extra careful. He spread the peanut butter perfectly. Then he added the pickles. But when he tried to add the spaghetti, it kept sliding off the bread. Long noodles dangled down like slimy worms. "Stay on there!" Rohan commanded the spaghetti. But it just slid onto the floor with a wet PLOP. His mom's cat, Whiskers, came running over and started eating the fallen noodles.

illustration.description: Rohan looking frustrated as long spaghetti noodles slide off his sandwich and onto the kitchen floor. A fluffy orange tabby cat is eagerly eating the fallen noodles. Rohan has his hands on his hips with an exasperated expression, while sandwich ingredients are spread across the messy counter.
illustration.alt: Spaghetti sliding off Rohan's sandwich while a cat eats the fallen noodles

pages.text: "Third time's the charm," Rohan muttered. He decided to skip the spaghetti and just use peanut butter, pickles, cheese, and banana. He carefully layered everything on the bread. It looked pretty good! But when he tried to put the second piece of bread on top, the whole thing was too tall. He pressed down hard and - SQUISH! Everything oozed out the sides. Pickle juice dripped on his shoes. A piece of banana shot across the room like a rocket. The cheese made a gross squelching sound. Rohan stared at his creation. It looked like a sandwich monster had exploded.

illustration.description: Rohan holding a completely squished and messy sandwich with ingredients oozing out all sides. His face shows dismay and his big eyes are wide with shock. Pickle juice drips onto his sneakers, a banana piece flies through the air, and the kitchen counter is covered in sandwich carnage. The lighting is warm but the scene is hilariously chaotic.
illustration.alt: Rohan holding his completely destroyed and messy sandwich

pages.text: Rohan arrived at the picnic with a regular peanut butter and jelly sandwich. His mom had helped him make it after the kitchen disaster. He felt disappointed. "This is so boring," he sighed to Maya and Carlos. "Everyone will think I'm not creative." The three friends sat on a red checkered blanket under a big oak tree. Other kids were already eating their lunches. Rohan could see sandwiches of all shapes and sizes. Some looked fancy and perfect. His looked... ordinary.

illustration.description: Three children sitting on a red and white checkered picnic blanket under a large green oak tree. Rohan looks sad holding his simple sandwich while Maya and Carlos sit supportively beside him. Other diverse children are scattered around the park having picnics in the background. The scene is bright and sunny with dappled sunlight filtering through the tree leaves.
illustration.alt: Rohan looking sad with his friends at the school picnic

pages.text: "Can I try a bite of your sandwich?" Maya asked. Rohan shrugged and handed it over. Maya took a big bite and smiled. "Mmm! This is really good. The peanut butter and jelly taste perfect together." Carlos tried a bite too. "Wow, this is delicious! My abuela always says the best food is made with love, not fancy ingredients." Just then, their classmate Zoe walked over. She had short red hair and was carrying a sandwich that looked like it came from a fancy restaurant. "What are you guys eating?" she asked.

illustration.description: Maya happily taking a bite of Rohan's sandwich while Carlos watches with interest. A red-haired girl named Zoe approaches carrying an elaborate sandwich. The children are sitting on their picnic blanket with expressions of joy and friendship. The warm afternoon light creates a cozy, welcoming atmosphere.
illustration.alt: Maya enjoying Rohan's sandwich while Zoe approaches their picnic

pages.text: "It's just peanut butter and jelly," Rohan said quietly. But Zoe's eyes lit up. "Really? I LOVE peanut butter and jelly! My mom always makes me these fancy sandwiches, but sometimes I just want something simple and yummy. Can I trade you half of mine for half of yours?" Soon, more kids came over. Everyone wanted to try Rohan's sandwich. "It's the perfect combination!" said Emma. "Not too fancy, just perfect!" added Marcus. Before long, Rohan was surrounded by friends, all sharing their different sandwiches and laughing together.

illustration.description: A group of diverse children gathered in a circle around Rohan, all happily sharing different sandwiches. Children of various ethnicities are smiling and laughing together on the grass. Rohan is in the center with a big happy grin, no longer looking sad. The scene is filled with warm golden afternoon light and shows the joy of friendship and sharing.
illustration.alt: Rohan surrounded by many friends sharing sandwiches and laughing together

pages.text: As Rohan walked home with Maya and Carlos, he felt happy and full. "You know what?" he said, grinning at his friends. "I think I learned something today. The best sandwich isn't about having the fanciest ingredients." "What's it about then?" Maya asked, adjusting her purple glasses. "It's about sharing it with the best friends!" Rohan replied. Carlos high-fived him. "And it's about not putting spaghetti on sandwiches," Maya added with a giggle. All three friends burst out laughing, remembering Rohan's kitchen disaster. "Next time I'll just stick to the classics," Rohan said. "But maybe I'll try pickles and ice cream!" "ROHAN!" Maya and Carlos shouted together, and they all laughed even harder.

illustration.description: Three happy children walking home together on a suburban sidewalk. Rohan is in the middle with his arms around his friends' shoulders, all three laughing heartily with big joyful expressions. The late afternoon sun casts long shadows and creates a warm, golden glow. Trees line the peaceful street and houses with colorful doors are visible in the background.
illustration.alt: Rohan walking home happily with Maya and Carlos, all laughing together

metadata.illustrationStyle: Playful storybook illustration style. Rich saturated colors. Expressive characters with big eyes. Whimsical, like Pixar concept art.

questions.question: What did Rohan want to put in his amazing sandwich?
questions.explanation: Rohan wanted to make an amazing sandwich with pickles and peanut butter, even though it sounded unusual!

questions.question: What happened when Rohan squeezed the peanut butter jar too hard?
questions.explanation: When Rohan squeezed too hard, peanut butter went flying and landed on his shirt, the counter, and even the ceiling!

questions.question: Who ate the spaghetti that fell on the floor?
questions.explanation: Whiskers, his mom's cat, came running over and started eating the spaghetti noodles that slid off the sandwich.

questions.question: What did the other kids think of Rohan's simple peanut butter and jelly sandwich?
choices: They loved it and wanted to share
questions.explanation: The other kids loved Rohan's simple sandwich and everyone wanted to try it and share their own sandwiches with him!

questions.question: What did Rohan learn about making the best sandwich?
questions.explanation: Rohan learned that the best sandwich isn't about fancy ingredients - it's about sharing it with the best friends!

pages.title: Complete Rohan's Kitchen Adventure
pages.instructions: Fill in the missing words from the story!
pages.text: Rohan wanted to make the most _____ sandwich ever for the school picnic. He planned to use _____ and peanut butter. But when he squeezed the jar too hard, peanut butter went _____ everywhere! The spaghetti kept sliding off like slimy _____. In the end, Rohan learned that the best sandwich is about _____ it with friends.

pages.title: Friends at the Picnic
pages.instructions: Choose the right words to complete the sentences!
pages.text: At the picnic, Rohan felt _____ about his simple sandwich. But Maya took a _____ and smiled. Soon, other kids wanted to _____ their sandwiches too. Rohan realized that _____ makes food taste better.
